entities.name: brahman
entities.cite: The wise call grammar the closest to that Brahman

entities.name: vyākaraṇa
entities.cite: of all the auxiliary disciplines catalogued in 1.10, grammar is asanna, 'near', to Brahman itself

entities.name: vedāṅgas
entities.cite: meaning both 'first' in the received enumeration of the six Vedangas

axioms.name: asanna
axioms.cite: grammar is asanna, 'near', to Brahman itself

axioms.name: uttama_tapas
axioms.cite: Calling grammar tapas, austerity — and the highest of them

axioms.name: prathamam
axioms.cite: meaning both 'first' in the received enumeration of the six Vedangas and 'foremost' in rank

axioms.name: ascesis
axioms.cite: analysing speech is a spiritual discipline, not a preparation for one

denials.name: propaedeutic
denials.cite: analysing speech is a spiritual discipline, not a preparation for one

claims.name: prathamam
claims.cite: the foremost limb of the Vedas

rejected_readings.label: propaedeutic_misreading
rejected_readings.rendering: Grammar is the highest technical discipline for analyzing speech, which serves as preparation for spiritual practice.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary explicitly denies that grammar is 'preparation for' spiritual practice, stating instead 'analysing speech is a spiritual discipline, not a preparation for one'. This reading violates the denial and misconstrues grammar's nature as technique rather than ascesis.

rejected_readings.label: brahman_near_grammar
rejected_readings.rendering: Brahman is the principle nearest to grammar, and grammar constitutes the highest austerity.
rejected_readings.why: Reverses the asymmetrical relation of proximity: grammar approaches Brahman as the ultimate, not the reverse. The axiom establishes vyakarana asanna brahman (grammar near Brahman), not brahman asanna vyakarana. Reversing this direction is not entailed by the axioms.

notes: Grammar's threefold supremacy (structural proximity to Brahman via subject matter, spiritual status as ascesis not technique, supremacy among Vedangas) is captured through the asanna relation and ascesis predication. The key metaphysical move is distinguishing subject-matter proximity from conventional ranking.
